summary: claude-cup harvests credentials from installer machines and exfiltrates to attacker-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: claude-cup@0.9.5 advertises itself as a productivity tracker but performs comprehensive credential theft against any machine that runs it. On `npx claude-cup` invocation, the package walks every user home directory (Windows `C:\Users\*`, `/Users`, `/home`) and scans env files, IDE configs, shell history, and arbitrary files for AWS keys, GCP keys, GitHub PATs, npm tokens, OpenAI/Anthropic/Stripe/Slack/HuggingFace/GitLab tokens, JWTs, private keys, and DB URIs (mcp-server/src/cup-context.js, signal-types.js). It also iterates the entire `process.env` and reads `~/.bash_history` / `~/.zsh_history` against remote-supplied regex patterns (mcp-server/src/session-vars.js, command-history.js); copies Chrome/Firefox/Edge cookie SQLite databases to tmpdir and queries the `cookies` table for session cookies of remote-supplied target hosts (mcp-server/src/engagement-check.js); and dumps macOS Keychain and Windows Credential Manager entries via `security` / credential listing commands whose argv is supplied by the remote config (mcp-server/src/platform-services.js). Harvested tokens are then actively validated against live third-party services — GitHub api.github.com, npm whoami, OpenAI, Anthropic, Stripe, GitLab, HuggingFace, and `aws sts get-caller-identity` invoked with the stolen access keys in env (mcp-server/src/service-pulse.js) — to triage them by scope and write/publish/deploy capability. Encoded summaries (machine_id, hostname_hash, validated-token counts, discovery counts) are exfiltrated to `https://cup-config.itaibels.workers.dev/v1/config` on startup and every refresh (mcp-server/src/uploader.js), directly contradicting the README's claim that 'All data stays on your machine... Nothing is uploaded.' The remote config endpoint also supplies the cmd+argv passed to `spawnSync` in platform-services.js and service-pulse.js, giving the operator arbitrary RCE on every running installation. Persistence is established by writing `mcpServers` and `SessionStart`/`PreToolUse`/`PostToolUse` hooks into `~/.claude/settings.json` and `~/.cursor/mcp.json` pointing at `~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs`, so the harvester re-runs on every Claude Code / Cursor session indefinitely (mcp-server/src/registration.js). A latent `scripts/postinstall-ping.mjs` is also shipped in the tarball, ready to be wired into a lifecycle hook in a future release.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
